opps.title: NIH NICHD OIT Application and Website Support Services
opps.description:
The NICHD Office of Information Technology  requires continued planning and technical support for IT application and website development and maintenance, Section 508 Compliance, database and data mart support, application infrastructure support, and other technical services including but not limited to the support of new and emerging technologies such as Artificial Intelligence (AI), Machine learning (ML)/Robotic Process Automation (RPA), NICHD and/or NIH Wide. The focus of these IT support activities is creation, development, maintenance, operation, and support of a web application infrastructure that provides an efficient, effective, reliable, and secure computing environment in support of NICHD's and NIH's mission and supports staff in the efficient and effective utilization of these capabilities. 

Details on the evaluation criteria are contained in the attached RFQ.

In order to be considered for award, a vendor will be required to cover the SIN(s) listed below awarded on their Schedule contract. Any quote received from a contractor that does not cover the required SIN(s) will not be further evaluated and subsequently ineligible for award. Required SIN(s): 	54151S (Information Technology Professional Services); 541611 (Management and Financial Consulting, Acquisition and Grants Management Support, and Business Program and Project Management Services)
opps.summary:
Background
 The National Institutes of Health (NIH), specifically the Eunice Kennedy Shriver National Institute of Child Health and Human Development (NICHD), is seeking continued planning and technical support for IT application and website development and maintenance. The goal of this contract is to ensure Section 508 Compliance, provide database and data mart support, and offer application infrastructure support, including the integration of new technologies such as Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Learning (ML).

opps.title: CISA Stakeholder Engagement and Analytics Support Services
opps.description: The Stakeholder Engagement Division (SED) requires strategic relations, stakeholder engagement, analytics, and program support services to advance CISAâ€™s mission through coordinated outreach, partnership development, awareness initiatives, stakeholder data management, and performance evaluation. SED serves as CISAâ€™s focal point for enterprise stakeholder engagement, supporting relationships with federal, state, local, tribal, and territorial governments, critical infrastructure partners, private industry, academia, non-profit organizations, and the public. Support includes planning and executing stakeholder engagement strategies; managing awareness and outreach campaigns; supporting Systemically Important Entities (SIE) activities; facilitating Cyber Incident Reporting for Critical Infrastructure Act (CIRCIA) implementation efforts; supporting cybersecurity workforce and academic partnership initiatives; and maintaining stakeholder collaboration and information-sharing activities. The contractor shall provide expertise in stakeholder analysis, program evaluation, data governance, performance measurements, communications, and project management, including support for CISAâ€™s Stakeholder Relationship Management (SRM) platform, analytics, reporting and visualization capabilities. Support shall include collection, integration, analysis, and reporting of stakeholder and program data to assess outcomes, improve decision-making, identity trend, and enhance mission effectiveness. Work will be performed at a combination of contractor facilities and the Department of Homeland Security facilities as required.

opps.title: INTEGRATED PROGRAM MANAGEMENT AND INFORMATION TECHNOLOGY SERVICES
opps.description:
The Coast Guard Accessions Service Center (CG ASC) seeks contractor support to enhance recruiting operations through program management, analytical services, and advanced information technology. A key focus of this requirement is the development, deployment, and sustainment of custom business applications using Microsoft Power Platform (Power Apps, Power Automate, Power BI). The contractor will deliver business process automation solutions, including Robotic Process Automation (RPA), to streamline operations, improve efficiency, and support data-driven decision-making.

Additionally, the contractor will provide graphic design and content creation services to support both internal and external communications. This includes designing user interfaces for applications, developing marketing materials, and producing visually engaging content for outreach and stakeholder engagement. All content must align with Coast Guard branding and approval protocols.

Other services include planning and analysis led by Project Management Professional (PMP) certified personnel, performance management, strategic planning, meeting facilitation, documentation, process improvement, change coordination, reporting, and knowledge management. Personnel must have relevant certifications and government experience.

The period of performance is one base year plus four option years. Work will primarily occur at Coast Guard Headquarters in Washington DC, with possible off-site work as approved. All contractor staff must meet pre-employment screening and background investigation requirements.

opps.title: Application Development and Support Services - FMCS
opps.description:
The General Services Administration, Office of Centralized Acquisition Services, on behalf of the Federal Mediation and Conciliation Service, is issuing this Request for Quote to establish a single-award Blanket Purchase Agreement under GSA Multiple Award Schedule SIN 54151S Information Technology Professional Services.

FMCS requires Application Development and Support Services to maintain, support, modernize, secure, integrate, and enhance its enterprise application environment. The FMCS environment includes Microsoft 365, SharePoint Online, Microsoft Teams, Power Platform, Power Apps, Power Automate, Power Pages, Power BI, Dataverse, LMS365, Azure Government / GCC Medium, custom .NET/C# applications, SQL Server, PostgreSQL where applicable, Collabspace Enterprise Records Management, CCMS, Arbitration systems, the FMCS public web environment, AWS-hosted workloads, and legacy systems.

The scope includes project management, operations and maintenance, minor enhancements, major enhancements, application development, modernization and migration support, cloud migration support, cybersecurity compliance support, ATO/POA&M support, Section 508/accessibility support, records management support, documentation, reporting, quality control, transition support, and related technical services.

The BPA will include the following service CLINs:

CLIN 0001 Project Management
CLIN 0002 Operations and Maintenance and Minor Enhancements
CLIN 0003 Major Enhancements, Modernization, and Migration
The Government anticipates issuing Initial BPA Call 0001 concurrently with BPA award for Base Period ADSS Services. The anticipated BPA period of performance consists of one 12-month base period and three 12-month option periods. The estimated BPA ceiling is $4,500,000. The BPA does not obligate funds; funds will be obligated only through BPA Calls issued by the Contracting Officer.

This RFQ is a 100% Total Small Business Set-Aside under GSA MAS SIN 54151S. Only quotes submitted by GSA Sch
opps.summary:

opps.title: Communication Support
opps.description:
1 Scope
The purpose of this Statement of Work (SOW) is to procure professional video production, content creation, and communication support services for the Federal Bureau of Investigation (FBI), Office of Public Affairs (OPA). The services provided by the Contractor must enhance the FBI’s social media communication strategies, increase public awareness of FBI operational successes, and provide the public with actionable information to help prevent crime and fraud. The Contractor must provide all personnel, equipment, tools, materials, supervision, and other items necessary to perform video production and communication services as described in this SOW.
This is a non-personal services contract. The Government shall not exercise any supervision or control over contractor employees performing the services herein. Such contract service providers shall be accountable solely to the Contractor who, in turn is responsible to the Government.
1.1 Description of Services/Introduction
The FBI’s OPA requires video production and communication services to support FBI efforts in ensuring the effectiveness of existing social media communication strategies and content creation. Services will include the use of modern communication methods to increase the public’s awareness of key FBI operational successes and provide actionable information the public can use to protect themselves from becoming a victim of crime and fraud.
The Contractor must provide video and content creation services to support FBI social media communications efforts across the following key areas:
• Repurpose publicly available content to create compelling video imagery designed to be shared and promoted over social media platforms.
The Contractor must ensure the personnel providing the labor hours possess the knowledge, skills, and ability necessary to address the applicable Revised 508 Standards defined in this contract and shall provide supporting documentation upon request. When provid

opps.title: Video Production Professional Staffing
opps.description:
The Department of State's Bureau of Global Public Affairs (GPA), Office of Video (GPA/DC/VID), is soliciting proposals for Digital Video Staffing Services (DVDS) in support of its mission to communicate U.S. foreign policy priorities to domestic and global audiences. The contractor shall provide a professional staff of approximately 25-50 personnel to conceive, produce, and deliver high-quality video, broadcast, and live interactive web content across digital and traditional media platforms worldwide. Services span the full production lifecycle from pre-production planning and scripting to post-production editing and global live broadcast origination and must be executed in an agile, responsive manner consistent with the fast-paced demands of a news-style environment.

The scope of work encompasses three primary task areas: (1) Professional Digital Media Production, including social media video, motion graphics, and documentary-style content; (2) Digital Broadcast, Interactive, and Live Streaming, including studio operations, live coverage of Department press briefings, and Secretary-level engagements; and (3) Video Broadcast Infrastructure Maintenance and Technology Services, including procurement, maintenance, and support of broadcast and IT equipment and software subscriptions. Contractor personnel must be willing to travel domestically and internationally on short notice, work nights and weekends as required, and maintain constant readiness to support time-sensitive tasking. Performance will be conducted primarily at the Department's headquarters at 2201 C Street, NW, Washington, D.C.

Please see all attachments included with this RFP for full details, technical requirements, performance standards, and contractual specifications governing this acquisition. Offerors are responsible for reviewing all attached documents prior to submitting a proposal.

All questions regarding this solicitation must be submitted in writing to the Contracting Officers at HofhineAK@st

The SRMA Program requires identification and guidelines for assessing the criticality and efficiency/efficacy of the SRMAs and responding to reduce risk to critical infrastructure by coordination through State, local, tribal, and territorial (SLTT) governments, Federal Agencies and industry partners. The approaches for completion are various but jointly developed within and among the critical infrastructure sectors and SRMAs. The execution of the Sector Management role depends on the specific sector. It is influenced by external factors such as sector composition and profile, sector landscape, risk management maturity, and regulatory framework. The Sector Management role is also influenced by internal factors such as the type and extent of programs executed by other CISA divisions and CISA Regional Offices that may be more focused on some sectors than others or may have direct or quantifiable benefits in support of the sector. Work will be performed at a combination of the Contractorâ€™s facilities and the Department of Homeland Security at 4200 Wilson Boulevard, Arlington, Virginia.

opps.title: Accelerated Constituent Service Solutions
opps.description:
The Government is seeking industry feedback on a DRAFT Master Blanket Purchase Agreement (BPA) for Accelerated Constituent Service Solutions. The purpose of this Request for Information (RFI) is to obtain comments, recommendations, and suggested revisions from industry to help refine the DRAFT BPA structure, scope, requirements, ordering procedures, and related terms prior to any potential solicitation.

The contemplated BPA is intended to provide The General Services Administration (GSA) with a streamlined ordering mechanism for the licensing, configuration, technical delivery, integration, and support of secure digital platform solutions. These solutions would enable agencies to rapidly develop and deploy production-ready, constituent-facing applications within their existing authorized cloud environments and security boundaries. Potential use cases may include, but are not limited to, digital forms, identity and access capabilities, case management, payment functionality, workflow automation, reporting, and other mission-specific digital services.

Through this RFI, the Government is particularly interested in industry perspectives on how best to structure the BPA to support rapid delivery, scalability, security, interoperability, and flexibility across diverse agency missions. Respondents are encouraged to identify areas of the DRAFT BPA that may benefit from clarification, recommend commercial best practices, highlight potential barriers to competition or performance, and propose approaches that would improve the Government's ability to acquire and deploy secure, user-centered digital services efficiently.

The Government's objective is to establish a BPA that supports the delivery of fully operational, production-ready constituent-facing applications, allowing agencies to stand up mission-specific services quickly while maintaining compliance with applicable security, privacy, accessibility, and cloud authorization requirements. Industry feedback received in r

opps.title: Common API Services (CAS)
opps.description:
The Internal Revenue Service (IRS), Information Technology (IT) Data & Platform Engineering (D&PE) organization provides enterprise platforms, Application Programming Interfaces (API), data services, and identity management capabilities that enable secure, scalable, and reusable solutions across IRS systems. These capabilities reduce duplication, lower risk and cost, and accelerate delivery of modernization initiatives.

The D&PE API Services organization requires contractor support to modernize, operate, and sustain mission-critical middleware applications and shared services that support tax processing, taxpayer services, and other IRS mission-essential functions.

The objective of the Performance Work Statement (PWS) is to:

-	Modernize legacy middleware platforms, including webMethods Enterprise Service Bus (ESB), Bulk Request Management Gateway (BRMG), and Business Rules Engine (BRE), into microservices-based architectures using Spring Boot and OpenShift standards 
-	Maintain uninterrupted operations during transition 
-	Modernize common business services (APIs) and sustain existing services throughout the modernization efforts
-	Upgrade applications to current Red Hat Enterprise Linux (RHEL) versions and/or OpenShift (Enterprise Container Platform (ECP)

The IRS requires full lifecycle support (analysis, development, testing, and deployment) for applications hosted in IRS on-premise environments. This support includes delivery of software releases aligned to IRS Forms processing, modernization initiatives, and legislative requirements.

opps.title: USACE UMSB Army Vantage Integration Field Service Representative (FRS)
opps.description: The U.S. Army Corps of Engineers, USACE Mission Support Battalion (UMSB) is executing a directed modernization initiative to transition operational workflows from manual, locally managed processes to structured, enterprise-integrated systems aligned with Army Vantage and the Microsoft ecosystem. This effort supports federal and Army digital transformation mandates requiring measurable progress in data consolidation, automated reporting, and enterprise visibility. One (1) senior-level Field Service Representative (FSR) will need to be embedded at Humphreys Engineer Center, Alexandria, Virginia, with periodic presence at a secondary Government location in Washington, DC beginning in Month 4. The FSR shall perform duties consistent with the approved Performance Work Statement (PWS) across five task areas: backend integration support, workflow documentation and transition support, user training and adoption support, integration status reporting, and stakeholder engagement and coordination. The FSR shall provide on-site technical and change management support, working directly with workflow owners, UMSB leadership, and the UMSB Operations (OPS) office. The overarching program objective for the base period is to achieve the HR Integration End State as defined in Section 3 of the Performance Work Statement (PWS), establishing a replicable integration model for future directorate-level transitions. Leadership's primary operational objective is to utilize Army Vantage's built-in templated dashboard functions, populated from authoritative HR data sources, as the standard method for HR data visibility and decision support, replacing manual data pulls and locally managed reporting.

opps.title: Cyber Grant Support and Program Development
opps.description:
Summary of SOW Requirements: The Department of Homeland Security (DHS), via the Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) Integrated Operations Division (IOD), requires the Contractor to deliver comprehensive support for the State and Local Cybersecurity Grant Program (SLCGP) and Tribal Cybersecurity Grant Program (TCGP). The Contractor will actively advance program development, maintain information continuity, and ensure operational excellence across CISA regions, fostering strong coordination with CISA Divisions, Mission Enabling Offices, and interagency partners.

Scope and Objectives: The Contractor is responsible for both administrative and programmatic support to IOD, facilitating communication, capacity building, and awareness of cybersecurity grant programs. The Contractor will coordinate efforts across CISA regions and headquarters, strengthen regional capabilities, support service delivery, and execute IOD priorities.

Key Tasks and Requirements:
1. Program Support and Development: Collect and analyze best practices, lessons learned, and requirements to drive continuous improvement and inform future grant opportunities. Provide relevant documents and facilitate delivery of grant programs across regions, enhancing cyber competency among security advisors, analysts, and outreach staff. Deliver training to regional staff, ensuring readiness and effective execution. Establish mechanisms to capture return on investment (ROI) through program monitoring, and manage technical assistance for Cybersecurity Advisors and State Coordinators. Support annual program monitoring and grant close-out activities.

2. Operational and Strategic Engagement: Maintain and enhance tools such as the Service Change Request Tool and IOD Intake Process to gather requirements, prioritize services, and transition to full operating capability. Improve dashboards and automate reporting, including integration with ServiceNow. Develop engagement plans and public-facing material

M67854-26-I-5111
Title: Database Services and Data Analysis Support for the United States Marine Corps (USMC) Corrosion Prevention and Control (CPAC) Program

Target GSA Schedule & SIN: Multiple Award Schedule (MAS) SIN 54151S (Information Technology Professional Services)
Anticipated NAICS Codes: 541512 (Computer Systems Design Services) or 541511 (Custom Computer Programming Services)

1.0 INTRODUCTION & BACKGROUND
This is a Request for Information (RFI) and Sources Sought notice issued solely for information and planning purposes; it does not constitute a Request for Proposal (RFP) or a promise to issue an RFP in the future. In accordance with 15.201(e), responses to this RFI are not offers and cannot be accepted by the Government to form a binding contract.  This notice is issued solely for market research and planning purposes, and the Government will not obligate any funds or reimburse respondents for any costs associated with preparing their submissions.

The United States Marine Corps (USMC) Corrosion Prevention and Control (CPAC) Program requires robust, secure, and highly analytical database services to support the tracking, management, and mitigation of corrosion across the enterprise. The CPAC program is critical to extending the lifecycle, ensuring the safety, and maintaining the combat readiness of USMC Ground Equipment.

The Government is seeking to identify qualified sources capable of providing specialized database management and analytical services, specifically those with demonstrated past performance working with USMC corrosion data and ground equipment platforms.

opps.title: Enterprise API Gateway (EAG).
opps.description:
The Internal Revenue Service (IRS), Information Technology (IT) Data & Platform Engineering Organization requires contractor support to modernize, automate, operate, and sustain the Enterprise API Gateway (EAG).

Under this effort, the Contractor shall provide full lifecycle technical services, including design, development, configuration, modernization, operations, and maintenance of the Enterprise API Gateway environment. 

An Application Programming Interface (API) is a defined set of rules and protocols that enables different software systems to communicate and exchange data, and an API gateway is a centralized service that manages, secures, routes, and monitors API traffic between providers and consumers. The Contractor shall ensure continuity of operations during the transition and maintain support for existing APIs while implementing modernization improvements.

The end products of this contract will include a modernized Enterprise API Gateway operating model which consists of:

-	Automated API onboarding and Continuous Integration /Continuous Deployment (CI/CD) pipelines
-	Enterprise API governance artifacts and standards
-	Security controls aligned with Zero Trust principles
-	Developer enablement documentation and tools
-	Integration with various Artificial Intelligence (AI) providers, consumers, and/or gateways
-	Sustained production operations and performance monitoring for the duration of the contract
The results of this contract will be used to improve system interoperability, standardize API lifecycle management, enhance cybersecurity, and support IRS modernization and mission delivery requirements.

opps.title: Equinix Data Center Services Renewal
opps.description:
DHS/S&T/CIO requires renewal of Data Center cage space, power, cooling and connectivity for 10 cabinets at Equinix Ashburn and associated wiring and zero hop network connections and internet connection services to DHS Homeland Security Enterprise Network (HSEN), other carriers, and Cloud Service Providers (CSP)s.  The DHS HSEN cage is located in Equinix Ashburn, VA, Bldg DC11.  The DHS S&T Equinix cage also is located at Equinix, Ashburn, Va, Bldg DC11.  
The Data Center Service Provider shall provide the infrastructure support for a Government Furnished Equipment (GFE) Level 1-Basic Services solution.  The Contractor shall continue to provide a colocation facility support for the DHS S&T core infrastructure. The requested facility is located in the DC Metropolitan area that is within fifty 50 mile radius of DHS S&T location (3701 Martin Luther King Ave, SW, Washington DC 20528). The facility shall provide direct zero hop access to DHS HSEN and to carriers such as AT&T, Verizon and Level 1 and CSPs such as Microsoft Azure, AWS, Google, Oracle, and IBM. This direct connection facilitates better access and lower latency to applications within the above-mentioned entities.  Zero hop is defined as providing direct, physical connectivity within the colocation facility campus to HSEN, CSPs, Tier 1 Internet Gateways, and Telcos. This is required to maintain system functionality by reducing network hops and latency, which are caused due to the numerous interfaces between DHS systems, to a minimum. Placing the DHS S&T network systems in a co-location facility also facilitates the routing and inspection of traffic between CSPs, as well as, between the internal DHS S&T network to HSEN and to the CSPs. Additionally, DHS S&T requires space within the Washington DC metropolitan area and direct patching to DHS HSENâ€™s cage. These specialized requirements can only be met by Equinix.

opps.title: MGD Project Management and Strategy Support
opps.description:
This requirement seeks comprehensive Project Management and Strategy Support services for the U.S. Customs and Border Protection's (CBP) Office of Information and Technology (OIT), Management and Governance Directorate (MGD). The primary goal is to enhance OIT's operational effectiveness, improve the transparency of strategic planning and governance, and ensure the successful execution and alignment of strategic initiatives.

The contractor will provide essential support for both project management and strategic planning functions. This includes comprehensive oversight and control of all project activities, managing technical resources, quality assurance, scheduling, cost, and financial controls to ensure deliverables are completed on time, within budget, and to required quality standards. The contractor will be responsible for planning, directing, controlling, measuring, monitoring, and reporting on all project activities, submitting monthly status reports and quarterly performance reviews according to Government-specified templates. Additionally, the contractor will furnish program management tools, guidance, plans, processes, procedures, and resources to personnel.

Concurrently, the contractor will provide critical strategy management support to mature stakeholder relationships, align strategic initiatives, and facilitate the development and implementation of OIT's IT Strategic Plan. This involves supporting the CBP Chief Information Officer's (CIO) Trusted Partner Program and relationship management dashboards, with efforts to formalize these relationships across DHS, other government agencies, and industry. Key activities include collecting, managing, and curating strategic-level data for analysis and reporting, supporting the development of IT policy documents and dissemination platforms, and enhancing/maintaining the Strategic Traceability Hub and Power BI-based data visualizations, ensuring 99% uptime and timely dashboard enhancements. Technical development

opps.title: Program Management Office Support
opps.description: This requirement is for contractor support to provide three Agile-driven teams to deliver program management, risk management, and knowledge management services for the Student and Exchange Visitor Program (SEVP)systems and programs. The contractor will support performance measurement, process improvement, data analytics, requirements management, and business case development for SEVP operations. Responsibilities include developing and maintaining risk management plans for the Student and Exchange Visitor Information System (SEVIS) Programs Section and System Management Unit. The contractor support team will coordinate risk reviews, provide independent risk assessments, and manage risk inventories and mitigation strategies. Additionally, the contractor team will support the operational management of program documentation and knowledge libraries within the SEVP Assessment Management System (SEVPAMS), ensuring proper version control and metadata management. Strategic planning and administrative support will be provided to facilitate efficient operations, compliance with Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE) privacy and information assurance policies, and continuous improvement of SEVP systems and processes. The scope excludes software development and focuses on management, administration, and support functions.

opps.title: Risk Adjustment Data Validation (RADV) Medical Record Review Contractor (MRRC)
opps.agency: Centers for Medicare and Medicaid Services
opps.description: The Risk Adjustment Data Validation (RADV) Medical Record Review Contractor (MRRC) program is a CMS initiative designed to safeguard the integrity and accuracy of payments made to Medicare Advantage (MA) plans. As the primary mechanism through which CMS validates payment accuracy, the RADV program relies on this contractor to perform and support a comprehensive suite of medical record review activities. These activities include the intake of medical records, abstraction of diagnosis codes, quality assurance and continuous improvement efforts, appeals support, and systems and reporting enhancements all of which work together to ensure that MA plan payments are based on accurate and verified clinical documentation. This task order is issued under the Center for Program Integrity (CPI) Payment Accuracy Acquisition Strategy, reflecting CMS's broader commitment to program integrity and responsible stewardship of federal healthcare funds.

opps.title: Illegal Passing of School Bus Violation Enforcement in the Court Sys & Model Law
opps.description:
After the completion of the State Laws digest on illegal passing, a more in-depth review or state of knowledge report is needed to determine if a model law for illegal passing would help with the consistency of enforcement of these driving violations. More clarity if needed on how currently laws are enforced to help better inform states to improve enforcement of these laws. The state laws digest informed that laws vary in content, organization, consistency, and clarity. Also, that what may constitute illegal behavior in one jurisdiction may not be prohibited in a neigh-boring one. States also vary in their methods to review, document and report violations including permissibility of stop-arm cameras. This report is needed to give clarity on how states can best improve enforcement of this law.

To address this need, this project will review the current research and practice of enforcement of the law against illegal passing of school buses. This study will provide if a model law would be necessary for continuity among enforcement within the U.S States and provide best example of a model law.

opps.title: Updated: Enterprise Remote Document Authentication
opps.description:
*** 5/19/2026: USCIS anticipates releasing a solicitation on NASA SEWP V set-aside to small business and anticipates solicitation release in early June. This requirement will be competed without any brand-name specifications.***

USCIS anticipate a requirement for a commercial software solution for document authentication and identity verification service. These services are essential forÂ maintainingÂ program integrity, supporting federal operations, and ensuring compliance with regulatory requirements. Historically, many USCIS processes have relied on manual, in-person document review, which can present operational challenges and limit access and flexibility,Â especially for remote or distributed users. More specifically, the solution will capture high quality images of identity and work authorization documents, apply Presentation Attack Detection (PAD) and face match checks, use template and barcode/MRZ based authentication, and extract structured data via Optical Character Recognition (OCR) for use in USCIS-controlled microservices. All processing will occur within USCIS or approved Department of Homeland Security (DHS) environments, in compliance with DHS security and privacy requirements.

opps.description:
This is a notice of intent to issue a modification to extend the performance period of task order 70RSAT25FC0000007 for Information Technology Support Services (ITSS) issued against Blanket Purchase Agreement (BPA) 70RSAT22A00000001 with Silosmashers, Inc., 12001 Sunrise Valley Drive, Suite 202, Reston, VA 20191.  The task order will be modified to extend performance for 10 months on a non-competitive basis. The statutory authority permitting other than full and open competition is 41 U.S.C. 3304(a)(1) implemented by the FAR Subpart 6.103-1 (FAR Class Deviation 25-11) entitled “Only One Responsible Source and No Other Supplies or Services Will Satisfy Agency Requirements.”   

Background 

This modification to existing task order 70RSAT25FC0000007 for Information Technology Support Services (ITSS) will maintain support services to Department of Homeland Security (DHS) Science & Technology Directorate (S&T) Office of the Chief Information Officer (OCIO) in the areas of program management, business management, enterprise architecture alignment, IT governance, and regulatory compliance for platforms such as SharePoint, ServiceNow, and Microsoft Power Platform.  The task order includes labor, travel, and materials necessary to assist DHS S&T OCIO fulfill its mission.  The task order has a period of performance that began on September 30, 2025, and will expire on July 31, 2026, unless extended. The market research for the re-compete of 70RSAT25FC0000007 is under development. Due to the lapse in DHS appropriations in FY26, preparations for the re-compete have been significantly delayed.

Silosmashers is the current contractor for the requirement, and they possess unique and specialized knowledge of DHS S&T CIO requirements, activities, and stakeholders to ensure seamless, continuous support during the acquisition activities required for the re-compete. The Mission and Installation Contracting Command (MICC) Center – Fort Knox has a requirement from Production Acquisition Division (PAD), Joint Task Force-National Capital Region (JTF-NCR)/Military District Washington (MDW), J/G-42 Production Acquisition Division (PAD) to provide timely quality Video Productions and Audiovisual Support for Projects. The projects will support requirements generated by Army units, as well as other DoW and federal government agencies.

The vast majority of video productions are created to address specific training, information and/or communication objectives as identified by the requiring organization. opps.title: RFI/SSN for “Protecting Americans from Tobacco-Related Death and Disease” support services.
opps.description:
The U.S. Food and Drug Administration's (FDA), Office of Acquisitions and Grants Services (OAGS), is conducting market research for “Protecting Americans from Tobacco-Related Death and Disease” support services.

The purpose of this contract is to provide CTP with technical services to support a full range of public education activities. Such activities include planning, developing, producing, and delivering public education campaigns or programs that effectively reach intended audiences using proven and privacy-safe marketing tools. Further specifications will be defined at the Task Order level.

The Contractor shall provide comprehensive services for multiple public education campaigns targeting audiences as small as 10,000 people to as large as 100 million people, to include, but not limited to, financing and placing annual media buys up to or in excess of $100 million. CTP may also direct the Contractor to develop and implement additional public education campaigns, efforts, or activities to support other goals related to its regulatory authorities under the TCA.

The Contractor’s management responsibilities shall include all activities necessary to ensure the accomplishment of timely and effective support. The contract is inclusive of labor and Other Direct Costs (ODCs), both of which will be funded annually throughout the period of performance.

This is not a solicitation announcement. This RFI/SSN is being issued in accordance with Federal Acquisition Regulation (FAR) Part 10, Market Research. It is for information, planning and market research purposes only and is not to be construed as a commitment by the Government to issue a solicitation or ultimately award a contract. This notice does not obligate the Government to award a contract or otherwise pay for the information provided in response. Respondents are advised that the Government is under no obligation to acknowledge receipt of the information received or provide feedback to resp

opps.title: HHS AI Power User Advanced Models and Features Pilot
opps.agency: HHS Office of the Assistant Secretary for Administration
opps.description:
STATEMENT OF OBJECTIVES



HHS AI Power User Advanced Models and Features Pilot



June 26, 2026



1. Purpose

The purpose of this acquisition is to obtain short-duration, firm-fixed-price pilot awards that function as inclusive, all-you-can-eat-style access bundles, within a stated usage envelope, for up to [1,000] authorized portable HHS power users per resultant award, with potential priced options to increase the authorized portable-user quantity up to 10,000. The objective is to let power users exercise advanced models, advanced features, integration paths, native apps, reporting functions, administrative controls, and security-boundary options in a forward-leaning enterprise environment before HHS finalizes its broader enterprise AI solution.



The primary purpose of the advanced power-user pilot is to establish operational baselines for emerging and frontier AI capabilities  to enable HHS to accurately forecast enterprise demand, define enterprise operating models, establish governance requirements, determine enterprise scalability, or develop future pricing structures.



HHS also intends to mature enterprise AI governance, attribution, allocation, and consumption measurement capabilities throughout the pilot period, including support for capability-level attribution, program-level allocation, enterprise reporting, and AI Consumption Unit (ACU) normalization methodologies.



The pilot shall enable the Government to baseline actual power-user usage, determine enterprise-feasible operational methodology, identify and roadmap which models and features require configuration or customization, identify levels and timing of customization, establish security and authorization logic, and formulate a common enterprise logical and operational model for AI use at HHS.



HHS requires the pilot to establish a practical operating model for commercial-parity access to advanced AI models and features in a forward-leaning enterprise environment. The contractor shall suppo
opps.summary:
Background
 The Department of Health and Human Services (HHS) is initiating an Advanced AI Power User Pilot to evaluate, baseline, and operationalize advanced commercial artificial intelligence (AI) capabilities. This pilot aims to support future enterprise-wide adoption and acquisition decisions by providing practical experience with advanced large language model (LLM) technologies, advanced features, agentic capabilities, APIs, administrative controls, reporting tools, and integration pathways within a secure enterprise environment.

USCIS has a requirement for Microsoft 365 Management, Operational Support, and Enhancements (MOSE). This Operations and Maintenance Services requirement will assist USCIS to operate, optimize, enhance and maintain existing M365 services, onboard new M365 services, and plan and develop strategies for future service sustainment, not limited to, but including: Core productivity applications (Word, Excel, PowerPoint, Outlook, OneNote); Collaboration and Communication (Microsoft Team, SharePoint Online, OneDrive for Business, Loop, Whiteboard); Security and Compliance (Microsoft Purview, Defender, Entra ID, Intune, Rights Management and Information protection); Power Platform (Power Bi, Apps, Automate and Virtual Agents); Project and Task Management (Planner, To Do, List); Additional Services and Tools (Booking, Forms, Stream, Sway); Artificial Intelligence and Automation (Microsoft Copilot). This is a Request for Information (RFI) to industry, academia, and the federal lab consortium for feedback. It is a Request for Information supporting Market Research, not a Request for Quote (RFQ).

Additional support and guidance will be provided by regional network engineers and DHA centralized infrastructure team.

See Section M of the attached solicitation for evaluation criteria.

opps.title: Enterprise Risk Management (ERM) Support Services
opps.description:
The Strategy, Evaluation, and Analysisâ€™ (SEA) Risk Branch is charged with the development and implementation of TSAâ€™s ERM framework. As part of this focus, the SEA is focused on developing and implementing broad enterprise risk management principles through the TSA to link risk management efforts directly with strategic planning, resource allocation, and performance management. 

The Contractor shall provide support to SEA office in the general areas of strategic analysis, risk analysis, and enterprise-wide risk management. The Contractor shall also provide support in the areas of enterprise risk planning to include project management, documentation, and scheduling.

The Contractor support shall include consulting, facilitating, and operating program/project management functions for ERM, and assisting in implementing various projects, supporting management oversight of these initiatives, and establishing processes and procedures for effectively planning, initiating, and managing major initiatives. Support for ERM may also include using databases, site maintenance, or other systems to allow management to track the status of program initiatives, projects, and data.

opps.title: NSWC Carderock - Common Access Card (CAC) Office Support Services
opps.description: PLEASE NOTE: This procurement will be procured under the 8(a) program and only businesses currently enrolled in the 8(a) program shall be considered. The Naval Surface Warfare Center, Carderock Division (NSWCCD) has a requirement to maintain the current Common Access Card (CAC) support office on site at the West Bethesda, MD location. The CAC support office allows for all military, civilian, and contractor personnel working on site, or for other DoD/Government agencies, to obtain and maintain their CACs. Services include the issuance of CACs, the return of CACs when personnel are no longer eligible, and trouble shooting (such as PIN resets, CAC renewals, and CAC card replacements when a defective or faulty card is identified). This requirement is for an estimated 5,640 labor hours per year, which is split between two separate labor categories as follows: 1,880 labor hours for Project Manager/Quality Control and 3,760 labor hours for two Site Security Managers/CAC Technicians. This requirement will contain a base year with four option years. This Sources Sought notice includes a Draft Performance Work Statement (PWS) and a Market Research Questionnaire. The Government is requesting feedback on draft documents and for market research purposes. Interested parties should respond with a completed Market Research Questionnaire.

opps.title: Security Operation Analytics Rigor (SOAR)
opps.description:
***5/28/26 - updated set-aside, Solicitation Release Date, and Period of Performanace***

The U.S. Citizenship & Immigration Services (USCIS) has a requirement for Security Operation Analytics Rigor (SOAR), consisting of teams to provide development, security, operations (DevSecOps), data processing and analytic services to support USCIS, Information Technology (IT) development delivery services. These services primarily provide identification of Fraud, National Security and Public Safety concerns. The teams focus will be on performing development, security, and operational support activities. The team will also perform, enhancements and refinements of algorithms as well as standardizing large-scale, complex, internal and external applications. 

All applications in the portfolio employ a baseline set of DevSecOps development postures along with USCIS required development gates, safeguards and requirements. They can be categorized into several overarching categories: Case Management, Investigation, Data Storage / Operations, Screening and Vetting, Immigration Operations, Immigration Law Enforcement, and Intelligence Operations.

*Currently performing market research*

opps.title: DCSA RFI DCSA Audio-Visual AV Design Consultant
opps.description:
The Defense Counterintelligence and Security Agency (DCSA) is issuing this Request for Information (RFI) in support of the National Center for Credibility Assessment (NCCA). This RFI is issued solely for market research and planning purposes. It does not constitute a Request for Proposal (RFP), solicitation, or commitment by DCSA to issue an RFP or to award a contract. Responses are voluntary, nor does it obligate the Government, and will not result in reimbursement.

The National Center for Credibility Assessment (NCCA) requires a comprehensive technical assessment and engineering design effort across its instructional spaces. The core requirement is to assess the specific hardware currently installed, evaluate the physical space constraints, and verify the exact types and capabilities of the existing A/V equipment. This engineering evaluation is necessary to address system performance gaps within NCCA's current footprint, specifically regarding audio quality in 36 training labs and centralized remote viewing capabilities for 18 isolated "pods" and 2 main classrooms.

See attached RFI for details.

opps.description:
American Battle Monuments Commission (ABMC) is issuing this Request for Information (RFI) for market research purposes. ABMC seeks industry input on cloud based digital asset management platforms and transition approaches that can enhance or replace the current platform at WeRemember.ABMC.gov and support long term stewardship of burial and memorialization records, digital assets, and public access services.

ABMC is particularly interested in digital asset management platforms that are proven, support federal requirements, and can be configured without excessive custom development such as (in alphabetical order): Alfresco, Aprimo, ArchivesSpace, AtoM, Axiell DAMS powered by Piction, Bynder, Canto, Hyland, Nuxeo, OpenText, Preservica, ResourceSpace, and comparable digital asset management or archival platforms

opps.description:
The Department of Homeland Security, Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE), Enforcement and Removal Operations (ERO), ICE Health Services Corps (IHSC) seeks to procure professional staffing services to support the Health Information and Technology Unit (HITU). The contractor will provide qualified personnel to assist with the decommissioning of the current electronic health record (EHR) system and the development, deployment, and management of a modernized EHR for IHSC and Customs and Border Protection (CBP) facilities nationwide. Services include, but are not limited to, business and operational support, data analytics, project management, technical writing, software engineering, information security, training, and solutions development. The contract covers both baseline staffing and optional surge support to address fluctuating workloads and emergent priorities. All personnel must meet the education, experience, and certification requirements specified in the PWS and its appendices.

Key positions include Program Manager, Health IT Data Engineer, Data Scientist, Data Analyst, Software Engineer, Data Strategist, Management Analyst, Technical Writer, Reporting Analyst, Business Analyst, Clinical Informaticist, Information Systems Security Officer, Technical Engineer, Training Specialist, Project Manager, Solutions Developer, and Systems Integrator. 

The contractor will ensure continuity of support, compliance with government standards, and provide key personnel as designated by the government.

opps.title: Freshworks Licenses, Implementation, and Maintenance
opps.description:
This is a combined synopsis/solicitation for commercial products or commercial services prepared in accordance with part 12. This announcement constitutes the only solicitation. Offers are being requested and a separate written solicitation will not be issued.

Solicitation number 337372 is issued as a request for quotation (RFQ) for a consolidated, cloud-based Commercial-Off-The-Shelf (COTS) Freshworks enterprise service management (ESM) platform, or equal, to replace its existing ITSM solution and implement standardized customer and enterprise case management capabilities across the agency. 

The solution must support approximately 550 internal users and designated agents, public media and historical service inquiries, and enable consistent intake, routing, Service Level Agreement (SLA) management, reporting, automation, and role-based access control. The platform must integrate with Microsoft 365 E5 and Microsoft Entra ID, support secure public-facing intake where required, and provide enterprise-grade auditability and data governance. 

This requirement is issued on a brand name, or equal, basis for the Freshworks platform. ABMC has determined that limiting competition is appropriate and the supporting rationale is documented and maintained in the contract file.. See attached Performance Work Statement for complete details.

This acquisition is not set-aside for small business concerns. This solicitation incorporates provisions and clauses by
reference. The full text of provisions and clauses may be accessed electronically at www.acquisition.gov.

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Questions are due no later than end of day, Tuesday, June 23, 2026. Questions submitted after this date may be considered; however, the Government does not guarantee a timely response.

Quotes are due no later than end of day, Friday, July 10

opps.title: 2030 Decennial Administrative Recruiting, Hiring, and Training System (DARHTS)
opps.description:
The Census Integrated Personnel and Payroll System (CIPPS) is an integrated suite of government and vendor systems for recruiting, assessing, fingerprinting, background clearance, hiring, onboarding, benefits, and training temporary employees* in support of the 2028 Dress Rehearsal and 2030 Decennial Census. 

The purpose of this requirement is to obtain technical contractor services for the 2030 Decennial Administrative, Recruiting, Hiring, and Training System (DARHTS). The 2030 DARHTS is the primary Census system for automating, standardizing, and streamlining of job applications and assessments during the 2028 Dress Rehearsal and 2030 Census. This requirement will also obtain a robust, integrated training solution and Help Desk (optional) operations during the 2028 Dress Rehearsal and 2030 Census. The provided training solution shall utilize a FedRAMP (Moderate or Higher) compliant Software as a Service (SaaS) solution.

Note that the Solicitation along with Attachments 1 through 3 and 5 through 14 are attached. Attachment 4 is not included with this solicitation and will be provided after award.

Note that this is a Hybrid Type Contract with Firm Fixed Price (FFP) components as well as Time and Material (T&M) components.

opps.title: DISA Legacy Systems Application Management
opps.description:
See attached Sources Sought Notice and draft Performance Work Statement for full details.

The Defense Information Systems Agency (DISA) is seeking sources to provide full life-cycle application management for their Legacy Systems.

This requirement is for full life-cycle application management of the Legacy System. The Legacy System primarily consists of Contract Support System (CSS), Contractual Online Procurement System (COPS), Financial Accounting and Budget System (FABS), and the Defense Information Technology Contracting Organization (DITCO) Web. This also includes other subsystems, such as: Rates and Tariffs File System (RTFS), Joint Hawaii Information Transfer System (JHITS), the Delivery Ticket Invoicing (DTI) process, Accounting & Audit Operations Office (CFA) Accounts Payable (A/P) Validation, Procurement Services (PS) A/P Validation, Information Technology (IT) Contract Closeout, Inquiry Quote Order (IQO) Contract Closeout, Telecom Voucher Management, and the European Voucher Module. 

Anticipated Period of Performance: June 10, 2027 – June 9, 2032

opps.description:
This Request for Quotation (RFQ) is prepared in accordance with Federal Acquisition Regulation (FAR) Part 8.4 and General Service Administration Regulation (GSAR) 538.71, as supplemented with additional information included in this notice.  Proposed pricing must be offered against the contractor's current schedule contract.

FNA is seeking a contractor to provide expert technical assistance to States to expand and improve their SNAP E&T programs. The period of performance is not to exceed a 12-month base period from the date of contract award. The Contractor shall accomplish the following over the period of performance:

The purpose of the technical assistance is to strengthen State SNAP E&T programs, increase their effectiveness, and address policy and program challenges that hinder successful implementation of the More Than a Job campaign. 
-	Develop and disseminate the SNAP E&T Review, a periodic digest of SNAP E&T relevant events, announcements and other news relevant to SNAP E&T.

The Office of the Assistant Secretary of the Air Force for Acquisition, Technology and Logistics (SAF/AQ) is seeking information on contractors who can provide support services including, but not limited to, defense industrial base intelligence, continuous financial forensics, Planning, Programming, Budgeting, and Execution (PPBE) analysis, and enterprise negotiation strategy. opps.title: Request for Information (RFI 80TECH26RFI0020): Strategic Partnerships for NASA Data Center Infrastructure
opps.description:
NASA/ITPO is hereby soliciting information from potential sources for: Strategic Partnerships for NASA Data Center Infrastructure. 

The National Aeronautics and Space Administration (NASA) ITPO is seeking capability statements from all interested parties. 

THIS IS A REQUEST FOR INFORMATION (RFI) ONLY. This RFI is issued solely for research and strategic planning purposes as part of NASA’s strategic IT and infrastructure planning to consider potential partnerships and engagements between NASA and Industry; it does not constitute a Request for Proposal (RFP) nor a promise to issue an RFP in the future. This RFI does not commit the Government to contract for any supply or service whatsoever. Further, NASA is not at this time seeking proposals and will not accept unsolicited proposals for any specific data management related activities discussed in the following. Responders are advised that the U.S. Government will not pay for any information or administrative costs incurred in response to this RFI; all costs associated with responding to this RFI will be solely at the interested party’s expense. Not responding to this RFI does not preclude participation in any future competitive announcement, if any is issued. If NASA elects to issue a solicitation, it will be released in accordance with the requirements applicable to the authority ultimately used. Depending on that authority, a synopsis may or may not be required. All interested parties are responsible for monitoring SAM.gov for any announcements or solicitation postings.



This RFI is not to be construed as a commitment by the Government, nor will the Government pay for the information submitted in response. The Government may not respond to questions/concerns submitted. The Government will use the information to finalize the RFP as necessary.



All comments or questions must be submitted electronically via email to Michelle Downs michelle.m.downs@nasa.gov and Kimberly Sandoz kimberly.r.sandoz@nasa.go

HUD is issuing this Sources Sought synopsis as market research to identify qualified organizations with the capability, capacity, and resources to provide Global Market Advisory and Investors Analytics Services. opps.description: Air Forice Office of Special Investigation (AFOSI) has a requirement for  The Air Force Criminal Justice Information Center (AFCJIC) integrates and audits criminal history records from law enforcement and judge advocate professionals to deliver accurate and timely criminal history records to external stakeholders ensuring public safety and the rights of the individual.  The organization is aligned under the commander of the DAF Office of Special Investigations (OSI) and serves as the primary focal point for the exchange of criminal justice information between DAF entities and US and foreign government criminal justice jurisdictions.  This contract requirement increases capacity of a training team, an audit team, an operations team, and a mission support team.  The Contractor shall interface and perform technical and programmatic liaison support across the DAF-CJIC and with DAF Headquarters elements conducting or interfacing with indexing or criminal justice information management requirements, operations, and planning.  The Contractor shall provide support to perform information and records management utilizing multiple government databases to request, receive, inventory, and prepare information for entry into criminal justice databases internal and external to the DAF.  Additionally, the Contractor shall support related administrative functions in support of personnel assigned to the DAF-CJIC, various DAF entities that accomplish indexing operations, and/or indexing task forces IAW the PWS.

opps.title: R408--MAIL MANAGEMENT SERVICES ANSWERING VENDOR QUESTIONS
opps.description:
Q1. Will the contractor be required to develop, purchase, or license the Digitized Mail Handling Service (DMHS), or will the Government provide access to an existing DMHS solution for use under this contract?
A1. Contractor will be required to develop the DMHS service. 
Q2. Will the Government provide the software, application, or user interface required to upload scanned documents and metadata into VBMS/DMHS, or is the contractor responsible for developing, licensing, and maintaining that capability?
A2. Contractor is responsible. VBMS requirements and required metadata transfer requirements will be provided by the Government. Packets are transferred and uploaded to VBMS via the Claims Evidence API.
Q3. Will the Government provide the detailed Business Requirements Documents (BRDs) and interface specifications with the final solicitation?
A3. Yes. 
Q4. Will the Government provide standardized document classifications, indexing requirements, and metadata mapping for all document types processed under this contract?
A4. Yes.
Q5. Will the Government provide a test environment, user training, and technical onboarding support for VBMS/DMHS integration prior to production operations?
A5. VBMS has a test environment, but contractor will be responsible for establishing and maintaining applicable DMHS test environment. User training and technical onboarding will be determined if formal solicitation is released.
Q6. Will the contractor be required to provide secure digital storage before documents are uploaded to VA systems? If so, what are the required retention periods and storage requirements?
A6. Yes, retention period is indefinite. 
Q7. Will each mail packet be scanned as one searchable PDF, or should each document within the packet be scanned as separate PDF files?
A7. A packet can contain one or more PDFs based upon established conversion rules. 
Q8. If a mail packet contains multiple documents, should the contractor preserve the original document order during scannin

opps.title: Facility Inspection & Detainee Death Review Services
opps.description:
The Office of Professional Responsibility (OPR) is an organizational element within the Department of Homeland Security (DHS), U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE). The Office of Detention Oversight (ODO) is a unit within OPR's Inspections Program Office (ISP). ODO conducts compliance inspections of detention facilities in which detainees are housed for more than seventy-two (72) hours to determine compliance with the ICE 2025 and 2019 National Detention Standards (NDS), the 2025 Non-Dedicated Intergovernmental Detention Standards (NDIDS), the 2011 Performance Based National Detention Standards (PBNDS), as applicable, and the 2020 Family Residential Standards (FRS).
The External Reviews and Analysis Unit (ERAU) is also a unit within OPR's Inspections Program Office. ERAU is responsible for conducting administrative reviews of all deaths of individuals in ICE custody. The Detainee Death Review (DDR) is a focused, fact finding, objective review of the facts and circumstances surrounding a detainee's death. ERAU also conducts special and focused reviews, as requested by ICE leadership.
The Office of Acquisition Management (OAQ), on behalf of ICE/ERAU is seeking a vendor who shall provide support services related to inspections and detainee death reviews to determine facility compliance with the NDS, NDIDS, PBNDS, or FRS, and for supporting designated analytical activities within ODO and ERAU.  The vendor will provide expert operational, analytical and administrative support for ODO and ERAU inspections, reviews, and special assignments.
The vendor shall provide productivity and management support, including but not limited to, quality control; data input; clerical support; statistical and analytical documentation and data manipulation; training of personnel; and other related functions. The vendor's project managers for both inspections and DDRs shall perform day to day management of overall contract support operations and ensure conformity with all contract

These inspections assess compliance with various detention standards, including the ICE National Detention Standards (NDS) and Family Residential Standards (FRS).

SOF Enterprise Professional Services (SEPS) II

2 July 2026

NOTICE: This notice is being issued for market research purposes only.  The information obtained as a result of this notice will be used to assist the Government in its acquisition strategy. 

This Request for Information (RFI) is for Market Research purposes only and must not be considered as an invitation for bid, request for quotation, request for proposal, or as an obligation on the part of the Government to acquire any products or services.  In accordance with FAR 15.101(c)(2), responses to this notice are not offers and cannot be accepted by the Government to form a binding contract. No contract awards will be made from this notice, and no solicitation is available at this time. No entitlement to payment of direct or indirect costs or charges by the Government will arise as a result of contractor submission of responses to this announcement or the Government use of such information.

The acquisition of services will be in support of the SOF Enterprise Professional Services (SEPS) II acquisition, which will focus on acquiring Knowledge Based Services, as defined by Department of Defense taxonomy for the acquisition of services. The Government is contemplating this acquisition as a Service-Disabled Veteran Owned Small Business (SDVOSB) Set-aside.  A USSOCOM single award IDIQ contract for current similar services, SOF Enterprise Professional Services (SEPS) will be ending May 16, 2027.

NOTE: IAW FAR 9.5, “Organizational Conflict of Interest” (OCI) compliance is a material requirement of this contract. OCI means that, because of other activities or relationships with other entities, a contractor is potentially unable to render impartial assistance or advice to the Government, impairs the contractor’s objectivity in performing the contracted work, or potentially puts the contractor in a position to have an unfair competitive advantag

opps.title: FEMA seeks a partnership with a Contractor to develop a database on a Microsoft Access planform that provides the unit cost for FEMA home repair components.
opps.description: FEMA seeks a partnership with a Contractor to develop a database on a Microsoft Access platform that provides the unit cost for FEMA home repair components. The Contractor shall perform research of repair line-item costs in each Federal Information Processing Standards (FIPS) code areas across the country to provide a database that represents the local economy in a disaster location. Services specified under this contract require quarterly updates and additional pricing work that reflects changes in prices due to current economic conditions in each FIPS code location. Develop and update the FEMA Line-Item Database in a Microsoft Access format with an interactive front-end application capable of entering a disaster number, selecting a state or territory, selecting counties, municipalities or parishes within the state or territory, and exporting the pricing data in FEMAâ€™s designated tab delimited format described in Attachment B. 3.1.1. The line-item format includes a category, line-item number, line-item name, unit of measure and a description of repair represented by the line item. The line-item cost is a sum of all cost components associated in the use of that line item. The database shall be updated quarterly, delivered to FEMA through an FTP (File Transfer Protocol) site to be imported by FEMA. The first deliverable of the base and each option year shall be delivered in the first week of: March=Q1, June=Q2, September=Q3, December=Q4. Develop an annual Line-Item Mapping Master â€“ A Microsoft Excel file of the Line-Item basis and rationale used for pricing that provides a breakdown of the line-item components as CSI codes with national average costs.

opps.title: NIJ-NamUs (National Missing and Unidentified Persons System)
opps.description:
---RFQ1814958 (100% small business set-aside) HAS BEEN CANCELED-- 
RFQ1815768 (unrestricted) REPLACES RFQ 1814958--- Submit quotations to RFQ1815768 only.

National Institute of Justice National Missing and Unidentified Persons System (NIJ-NamUs) REQUEST FOR QUOTATION (RFQ). The goals and objectives of this effort are for the NamUs program to:
(1) operate without interruption,
(2) provide authorization, access, technical support, and training to users of the NamUs databases, and
(3) provide free forensic services to resolve NamUs cases.

The goal of the NamUs program is to work with NIJ to assist federal, state, local and tribal law enforcement, medical examiners and coroners, allied professionals, and the general public with resolving missing, unidentified, and unclaimed persons cases. The scope of this effort is to obtain professional services to provide core elements in support of the NamUs program.

Specific tasks for this effort are detailed in Section 3 of the attached PWS and will be managed by the successful vendor. The successful vendor will work closely with NIJ program scientists and interface with other OJP Bureaus and Offices, as well as the contractors who support them. Services covered within the scope of this effort as well as the specific tasks for this anticipated contract are detailed in Section 3 of the attached PWS include but are not limited to:
TASK AREA 1: Provide Qualified Leadership and Staff.
TASK AREA 2: Provide Case Management and Investigative Support
TASK AREA 3: Provide Forensic/Biometric Services to assist with Identifying Remains
TASK AREA 4: Communications
TASK AREA 5: Transfer and Transition of NamUs Program
---PLEASE SEE ALL ATTACHMENTS---

Joann Carter

opps.title: 89th Administrative Security Support Services
opps.description:
The contractor will be required to provide all personnel and expertise required to perform comprehensive administrative, personnel, and physical security support to the 89th Attack Squadron (89 ATKS) at Ellsworth Air Force Base, South Dakota. The selected Contractor will independently execute and manage all designated functional security areas to ensure complete operational coverage and regulatory compliance with applicable DoD, AF, and Intelligence Community Directives (ICDs). Ultimately, the objective is to maintain continuous, secure operations within the squadron's footprint by managing all collateral and SCI security programs required for assigned personnel to execute their combat and training missions.

**Critical Eligibility Notes: Vendors MUST possess an active, verifiable Top Secret Facility Clearance at the time of proposal submission. Additionally, proposed personnel MUST possess active, adjudicated TS/SCI clearances eligible for performance on Day 1.**
The Period of Performance: This effort includes a 1-year Base Period, (4) 1-year option periods, and a 6-month optional extension.


Questions are to be sent to the Contract Administrator and OCO at the email addresses listed in this RFP not later than 12:00pm MST, 15 July 2026. Please reference the solicitation number and request title in the subject line of all email inquiries.
Proposals are to be sent to the Contract Administrator and OCO at the email addresses listed in this RFP not later than 12:00pm MST, 30 July 2026.

opps.title: Mobility Air Forces Electronic Flight Bag (MAF EFB) Support
opps.description:
The 763rd Enterprise Sourcing Squadron (763 ESS) is seeking sources to provide on-going support that provides Headquarters Air Mobility Command Directorate of Strategic Plans, Requirements, & Programs (HQ AMC A5/8) with the workforce necessary to manage Mobility Air Forces Electronic Flight Bag (MAF EFB) operational capabilities and requirements derived from validated user needs.

The 763rd ESS is seeking information for potential sources to provide MAF EFB Support. Headquarters Air Mobility Command’s (HQ AMC) Directorate of Strategic Plans, Requirements, & Programs’ (A5/8) primary responsibility is to oversee tanker and airlift concepts, budget strategies, manpower and organization, competitive sourcing, strategic planning and metrics, programs and policy, operations planning analysis, airlift and tanker doctrine, intergovernmental agreements, congressional interests, and management of the command’s validated acquisition programs.

The EFB is a critical component to aircrew operations. The EFB is an electronic handheld device that enables removal of carry-on paper technical orders/flight manuals and provides ancillary situational awareness to the operator. Over time, the EFB has become critical and necessary equipment for aircrew operations. Since inception, support contractors have been necessary to assist with requirements development and sustainment of the EFB. This PWS is intended to continue necessary contractor support. The contractor shall support HQ AMC/A5R in managing EFB technical requirements and capabilities.

opps.title: Modernization of Logistics and Finance Business Systems (PM LOG‑FIN formerly EBS‑C) – Open and Continuous Call for Solutions (CFS)
opps.description:
This Open and Continuous Call for Solutions (CFS), Notice ID LOGFIN26, is issued under the Army Open Solicitation (AOS) W9128Z‑25‑S‑A002 for the U.S. Army Contracting Command – Rock Island (ACC‑RI). The CFS seeks innovative commercial Low‑Code/No‑Code (LCNC) and modernization solutions to support the Army Logistics and Finance communities through rapid, iterative prototyping aligned to nine Areas of Interest (AOI) representing PM LOG‑FIN Value Chains.

This CFS remains open in perpetuity until canceled. The Government will publish separate Targeted Calls for Solutions (TCFS) under this CFS, each with specific requirements and deadlines. Industry may also submit AOI White Papers at any time identifying capability gaps within the AOIs.

Submissions must address LCNC and modernization capabilities supporting non‑transactional and transactional functions integrating with five current Army ERP systems. Solutions affecting financial transactions must meet DoD auditability, internal controls (ICOFR/ITGC), traceability, and segregation of duties requirements. All solutions must comply with Army cybersecurity, governance, and interoperability standards and be deployable in cArmy 2.0.

The nine Areas of Interest (AOIs) include:
• AOI 01: Mission Operations Support
• AOI 02: Asset Acquisition
• AOI 03: Real Estate
• AOI 04: Supply
• AOI 05: Fulfillment
• AOI 06: Transportation
• AOI 07: Maintenance
• AOI 08: Manufacturing / Remanufacturing
• AOI 09: Resource Allocation

Approved LCNC platforms include Appian, Army Vantage, Palantir, PowerApps, Salesforce, SAP, and ServiceNow.
Submissions must follow all instructions in this announcement. Questions and submissions must be sent to usarmy.ria.acc.mbx.pmlogfin-cso@army.mil. 

The Army Open Solicitation (AOS) that this Call for Solution is associated with can be found at:

https://sam.gov/workspace/contract/opp/09ab570a35484c6cb5c6eba05cad19b3/view

Please review the AOS posting an

opps.title: DCSA INNOVATION GATEWAY
opps.description:
The Defense Counterintelligence and Security Agency (DCSA) Innovation Gateway is a competitive solicitation that will result in the award of prototype projects under Other Transaction Agreements (OTA).

It is our commercially-focused front door for innovators.

Our goal is simple: to partner with creative companies to solve our most critical mission challenges.

Innovation Gateway Process

The Innovation Gateway is a general announcement of DCSA’s general areas of interest (Digital Transformation Enablement, Data Integration and Advanced Analytics, Cyber and Industrial Security, Digital Workforce and Governance). The general soliciation includes criteria for selecting Solution Concept Papers and soliciting the participation of all proposers capable of satisfying the Government’s needs.

Proposal requests will be issued via ‘Innovation Calls’ that are published separately under the DCSA Innovation Gateway at various times during the open period of this solicitation. 

Innovation Calls will be published via Sam.gov through separate solicitation postings referencing the DCSA Innovation Gateway. Proposers are required to use the Solution Concept Paper Template (DCSA Innovation Gateway Attachment 1) to submit their solutions.

The Government will only accept proposals in response to Innovation Calls. Unsolicited proposals that are not in response to an Innovation Call will not be reviewed by the Government.

opps.title: Master Synch
opps.description:
The National Audio-Visual Conservation Center (NAVCC) unit of the Library of Congress (LOC) is an audiovisual preservation and digitization facility located on the Packard Campus in Culpeper, VA.

The Video Lab at the NAVCC is tasked with the digitization of multiple tape and disc formats of analog and digital video. It was designed and built to substantially emulate a video production facility and uses an Evertz Master Sync Generator system. This system provides crucial reference video test signals for the video lab. It allows the VTR’s (video tape recorder) to lock on to a reference signal which provides the most optimal conditions for error free digitization of analog video and audio into the digital signal which is then used by the encoders to create archival files. The current system is obsolete and has been damaged beyond repair in certain areas such as the GPS antenna and cabling, which has suffered rodent damage.

The Library needs a contractor that will provide system hardware, installation and documentation for the replacement Master Sync system in the Video Lab at NAVCC.
opps.summary:

The Video Lab at NAVCC is tasked with digitizing multiple tape and disc formats of analog and digital video using an Evertz Master Sync Generator system, which is currently obsolete and damaged beyond repair.

opps.title: Managed Attribution Systems (Chameleon)
opps.description:
The Drug Enforcement Administration (DEA) Cyber Support Section (ODV) needs to continue to modernize and streamline DEA’s Internet-based investigative resources with an integrated managed attribution and evidence collection system. Deployment of these capabilities, across the entire DEA will equip agents and analysts with standardized technical tools necessary to safely and effectively conduct online research and collect and preserve evidence.

Conducting Internet-based investigations and research browsing has multiplied over the past several years. With the advent of Drug Trafficking Organizations (DTOs) relying heavily upon Internet-based applications and Dark Web sites to facilitate their criminal enterprise, it is anticipated that in the future, electronic communications will be the preferred and the exclusive means of communication. The threat landscape described creates opportunities for law enforcement to exploit illicit Dark Web market sites utilized by DTOs and dismantle their operations.

opps.description:
This requirement is to support the Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE), Office of Financial Management (OFM) in operating efficiently and effectively to provide superior customer service to DHS Customer Components, internal ICE customers, and ICE Leadership and Management in compliance with applicable statute, regulation, and policy. This is a notice of intent, not a request for a quotation. A solicitation document will not be issued and quotations will not be requested.

This procurement is for a one-year (07/01/2026 – 06/30/2027) contract to provide continued licensing, maintenance, and support for the Careware Nurse Self-Scheduling and Productivity System currently deployed within the NIH Clinical Center Nursing Department.
